project.title: The cargos and roles of small extracellular vesicles from cochlear explants in cisplatin-induced ototoxicity
project.projectDescription: Small extracellular vesicles (sEVs) play an important role in intercellular communication as well as pathological development. Here, to explore the roles of sEVs in the progression of cisplatin-induced ototoxicity, we isolated sEVs from the conditioned culture medium of cisplatin-induced cochlear explants (Cis-sEVs) and the control group (Ctrl-sEVs) and profiled their cargos using label-free LC-MS/MS. Numerous proteins involved in the regulation of the damage response were enriched in the Cis-sEVs. Cltc, Cct2, and Hspa8, which are potentially involved in protein homeostasis and autophagy, are up-regulated in Cis-sEVs compared to Ctrl-sEVs but not in cisplatin-damaged cochlear tissue or in HEI-OC1 cell lysates.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: The dissociation and culture of P3 cochlear explants was cultured with 2 ml DMEM/F12 medium (Gibco, 11330-032) supplemented with 10% (v/v) fetal bovine serum (FBS; PAN biotech, ST30-3302) and 0.05% (v/v) ampicillin at 37 ℃ with 5% CO2. The cochlear explants treated with 50 μM cisplatin or an equal volume of PBS for 48 h were used to build an ex vivo cisplatin-induced ototoxicity model.The sEV-free FBS medium was used to culture the cochlear explants. The conditioned culture supernatant was collected and stored at 4℃, and sEVs were isolated from the supernatant within one week.A series of sequential centrifugation steps were employed to remove cells and cellular debris, including 600 × g for 10 min and 2000 × g for 20 min at 4°C. To remove large vesicles, the supernatant was then filtered through a 0.22 μm filter (Millipore, SLGPR33RB) and centrifugated at 16500 × g at 4°C for 20 min. After concentrating with a 100 kD molecular weight cut-off ultrafiltration device (Pall, Shanghai, China), the sEVs were precipitated by ultracentrifugation at 110,000 × g at 4°C for 2 h using a Himac CP-90NX ultracentrifuge (Himac, Japan). After removing the supernatant, the pellet was resuspended and ultracentrifuged at 110,000 × g for another 2 h to further remove any remaining contaminants. Lastly, the purified sEV pellet was resuspended in 200 μl D-PBS and then stored at –80°C for further use. To explore the differentiation of protein molecular profiles between the Cis-sEV and Ctrl-sEV groups, small RNA-seq analysis was performed with three biological replicates in each group. The sEVs isolated from around 50 cochlear explants from each sample were used for lable-free LC-MS/MS.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Protein extraction and label-free LC-MS/MS analysis were performed by the Beijing Genomic Institute (BGI) (Beijing, China), and the data were analyzed on the Dr. Tom network platform. Briefly, the digested peptides separated by HLPC (UltiMate 3000) (Thermo Fisher Scientific) were detected using a Q-Exactive HF X (Thermo Fisher Scientific, San Jose, CA), and the data were evaluated using the MaxQuant 1.5.3.30 system integrated with an Andromeda engine performing the search in the Uniport database. A false discovery rate (FDR) ≤ 1% was used for mass filtration at both the spectral level and the protein level so as to obtain significant identification results. MaxQuant was used to extract the intensity of the peak area and thus quantify the protein expression level.

references.referenceLine: Ai J, Zhang S, Dai M, Jiang P, Huang J, Xiao H, Lin Y, Tang X, Tong W, He J, Mao Q, Wang Y, Ye Z, Wang T, Chai R. Small Extracellular Vesicles Orchestrate Cisplatin-Induced Ototoxicity: Potential Biomarker and Targets Discovery. Adv Sci (Weinh). 2025 12(30):e02627
